Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form
Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell
Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs
Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Reference DOM (Deep wrapped label): Label without for wraps input three div levels deep Reference DOM (Optgroup select): Department select uses optgroup for grouping options Reference DOM (Table layout form): Form laid out as HTML table with labels in td cells Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: glued-label-text, decoy-form, iframe-apply, pill-yes-no, shadow-host. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: definition list dl/dt/dd label pairs. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Deep wrapped label; Optgroup select; Table layout form.
